name: pool-route
description: PreToolUse(Bash) pool auto-router + SessionStart routing-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pool_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has ‚Č•1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the heavy classifier (make ¬∑ cargo ¬∑ npm ¬∑ gradle ¬∑ pytest ¬∑ go build ¬∑ swift build ¬∑ docker build ¬∑ nvidia-smi ¬∑ train ¬∑ hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build ¬∑ aprime_cc ¬∑ `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan ¬∑ ‚Ä¶) or a root-needing command, the command is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field to run on a pool host over ssh (`ssh <host> 'cd <workdir> && <cmd>'`) ‚ÄĒ transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only (0.6.0): the 0.5.7 load-escalation gate ‚ÄĒ which promoted any non-trivial cmd to heavy when summed non-claude CPU exceeded 150% ‚ÄĒ was removed because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and shipped local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (0.6.0, extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`¬∑`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`¬∑`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local ‚ÄĒ the dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`¬∑`~/.hexa-cache`¬∑`~/.pool`¬∑`~/.sidecar`¬∑`~/.config`¬∑‚Ä¶) are per-host hidden state that is NOT user-synced, so routing them to a pool host reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs are excluded and keep routing (the `find ~/core/anima` big-tree pattern still dispatches). Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local ‚ÄĒ the literals resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them to a pool host fails (cache absent). Trusted surface (sidecar slash commands like `/quota:quota`, `/pool:pool`, `/check`, `/end`, `/ship`, `/inject`, `/secret`, ‚Ä¶ all match by writing convention @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate (0.6.4): within the absolute-path exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb ¬∑ direct `hexa.real` ¬∑ python/python3/py ¬∑ `bash`/`sh <script>`) now require a fresh `local` sign-off token ‚ÄĒ they were the canonical mac fork-storm trigger (multi-session dispatch-cache races + re-fork on every env-hash miss). USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt (5min token, mints freely via `!` bang); the token file `~/.sidecar/signs/local.sign` holds the mint epoch. Without a fresh token the hook emits `permissionDecision: deny` with an instruction trailer. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free ‚ÄĒ the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. HOTFIX 0.6.5: (a) `_local_heavy_interp` now matches FIRST-TOKEN basename instead of `_has_word("hexa")` ‚ÄĒ 0.6.4 mis-matched `hexa` inside path strings like `/Users/.../hexa-lang/foo.txt`, gating bare reads like `cat <path>`. (b) `hexa run` + `hexa build` added to the heavy classifier ‚ÄĒ 0.6.4 missed them, so they ran local on the mac instead of routing to the pool (= mac fork-storm trigger when multi-session). The two together close the false-positive AND false-negative paths. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 ‚ÄĒ supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now AUTO-DISPATCH candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build ¬∑ xcodebuild ¬∑ codesign ¬∑ ‚Ä¶). The 0.6.1 blanket exclusion left compile-heavy work (swift-frontend can drive 85%+ CPU on the workstation) stuck on the local Mac even when an enabled `mini` was idle. New policy: (a) macOS-only commands route to macOS pool hosts; (b) Linux-only commands route to Linux pool hosts (unchanged); (c) general-heavy commands round-robin across Linux pool hosts only ‚ÄĒ the workstation Mac stays protected from generic build/test fan-out and is reached only when a macOS-capability marker explicitly demands it. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + `hexa <subverb> --help` dispatchable for kick/drill/loop/cc), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown ‚ÄĒ never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local ‚ÄĒ it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool (needs the local hexa build + stdlib/cloud), and its post-`--` remote argv routinely carries heavy words (nvidia-smi ¬∑ train.log ¬∑ make) that tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux pool host with no `cloud` subcommand. Double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. Worktree‚Üícanonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x ‚ÄĒ the standard stdlib/SSCHA isolation pattern) previously DENIED ('cwd outside $HOME'); it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` (listed first) and, when that root is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir), mirrors it instead. This runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command ‚ÄĒ it strictly rescues worktree dispatch. OS-capability filter + round-robin load balance. NO opt-out by design.
